title: All Other Legal Services

alternatives.name: Legal filing, search, and managed paralegal-support provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm classified in NAICS 541199 whose primary controlled service is legal or patent document filing and search, docket or record retrieval, or managed paralegal support.
Operates as a non-attorney legal-practitioner or paralegal-services organization rather than an office of lawyers, title abstract office, or real-estate settlement office.
Estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band after market-rate owner replacement compensation.
exclusion_criteria: Office of lawyers or attorneys classified in NAICS 541110.
Title abstract or real-estate settlement office classified in NAICS 541191.
Firm primarily providing field process service, stand-alone notarization, or specialized practitioner and advisory services assigned to another enumerated archetype.
Nonemployer practitioner and government-operated legal service.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 26.2% of NAICS 541199 receipts in legal and patent document filing and search and 19.8% in other legal support services. Applying the combined 46.0% receipts share to the normalized 174-firm band count gives 80. The low applies the larger of the two reported establishment-incidence shares, 23.6%, to the band count and rounds to 41; the high retains the additive receipts-share allocation because establishments can report both product groups and no published table identifies each firm's primary product within the target band.

alternatives.name: Specialized non-attorney practitioner and legal-advisory support provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm classified in NAICS 541199 whose primary controlled service is substantive non-attorney legal-practitioner, expert, mediation, or advisory support.
May support estate, family, real-estate, commercial, civil, employment, or other legal matters without operating as an office of lawyers.
Estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band after market-rate owner replacement compensation.
exclusion_criteria: Office of lawyers or attorneys classified in NAICS 541110.
Firm primarily providing filing and search, managed paralegal operations, field process service, or stand-alone notarization assigned to another enumerated archetype.
Arbitration or conciliation establishment outside the 541199 boundary under the NAICS cross-reference.
alternatives.rationale: The base applies the combined 37.8% receipts share of the top-level criminal, wills and estates, family, real-estate, business and commercial, civil-negligence, labor and employment, other civil, expert-witness and legal-consulting, and arbitration or mediation product rows to 174 and rounds to 66. The low applies the largest single establishment-incidence share within the group, 23.2% for wills, estates, and trusts, and rounds to 40. The grouped establishment incidences sum above 100%, proving material multi-product overlap but not a tighter union; the defensible upper bound is therefore the entire 174-firm band. The wide range also preserves uncertainty created by product reporting that appears broader than the official non-attorney NAICS boundary.

alternatives.name: Process-service and field legal-logistics provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm classified in NAICS 541199 whose primary controlled service is service of process or closely related field legal logistics.
Coordinates field agents, attempts, affidavits or proofs of service, status reporting, and client delivery.
Estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band after market-rate owner replacement compensation.
exclusion_criteria: Private investigation firm primarily classified outside NAICS 541199.
Firm primarily providing filing and search, managed paralegal support, specialized practitioner support, or stand-alone notarization.
alternatives.rationale: Process-server services account for 11.0% of industry receipts and are reported by 15.0% of establishments. Applying those two disclosed shares to the 174-firm band gives the supported range of 19 to 26, with the receipts-share allocation of 19 as base.

alternatives.name: Notarization and certification service provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm classified in NAICS 541199 whose primary controlled service is notarization or document certification.
Provides the service through employed or contracted commissioned practitioners and supporting scheduling, identity, document, and record controls.
Estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band after market-rate owner replacement compensation.
exclusion_criteria: Incidental notary activity inside a bank, law office, shipping store, government office, or other establishment classified outside NAICS 541199.
Firm primarily providing filing and search, managed paralegal support, specialized practitioner support, or process service.
alternatives.rationale: Notarization and certification services account for 4.3% of industry receipts and are reported by 15.0% of establishments. Applying the disclosed shares to the 174-firm band gives 7 to 26, with the receipts-share allocation of 7 as base.

residual.name: Ancillary, mixed-primary, and unclassified 541199 providers
residual.rationale: The four base receipt-share allocations total 98.9% and 172 of 174 band firms, leaving two as the named residual. The low allows complete allocation after rounding. The high preserves up to the 38.5% gap between base archetype coverage and the sum of the four narrowest supported lower allocations, reflecting multi-product reporting, suppressed minor product receipts, and classification ambiguity.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System, United States, 2022
2022 Economic Census: Industry by Products Statistics for the United States
2022 Statistics of U.S. Businesses Annual Data Tables by Establishment Industry
2022 Economic Census: Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services Summary Statistics

target_archetype.selection_basis: The frozen base allocation applies mutually exclusive top-level product-receipt group shares to the normalized 174-firm target band. Filing, search, and managed paralegal support has the largest base at 80; specialized practitioner and advisory support is second at 66; process-service field logistics is 19; and notarization and certification is 7. The selected base therefore follows the largest-count rule. The top two ranges overlap because the Economic Census publishes product receipts and product incidence, not firms cross-tabulated by primary product and EBITDA band. Selection uncertainty is true, coverage and value-basis treatment preserve that uncertainty, and the record remains one selected archetype only.

value_basis.scope: Recurring operations of the selected legal filing, search, and managed paralegal-support provider after market-rate owner replacement compensation.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: R_cva equals recognized revenue on a consistently stated accounting basis less only documented third-party pass-through amounts contractually or economically owed to outside providers and not controlled as value added by the operator. A cost deducted from revenue may not also enter employee or controllable-contractor cash cost.
approved_pass_through_categories: Government, court, registry, and intellectual-property-office filing, search, examination, issue, publication, recording, and maintenance fees separately identified and billed through at documented actual amounts
Third-party official-record, certified-copy, registry, and statutory-publication charges separately identified and billed through at documented actual amounts

Outside local or foreign agent, process-service, courier, and analogous fulfillment charges only when the client contract and invoice establish exact reimbursement, no operator pricing discretion, and no operator-controlled value added

roles.title: Employed paralegal, filing, search, docket, and document-delivery staff
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for employees whose primary function is controlled matter intake, filing preparation and submission, legal or patent search, docket maintenance, record retrieval, document processing, and delivery.

roles.title: Employed quality, exception, project, and client-service staff
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for employees whose primary function is review, exception resolution, deadline control, project coordination, client communication, and service-level administration.

roles.title: Employed management, commercial, finance, technology, and administrative staff
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for employees whose primary function is general management, sales and account development, finance, people operations, internal technology, compliance, facilities, or administration.

roles.title: Controllable contract paralegal, filing, search, and document specialists
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for recurring delivery contractors whose work is directed and quality-controlled by the operator and is not an exact client-reimbursed pass-through.

roles.title: Controllable local-agent and specialist network
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for local agents and specialist practitioners when the operator controls scope, routing, quality, and pricing; exact reimbursed third-party charges remain outside this bucket under the pass-through boundary.

roles.title: Controllable contract technology, data, and overflow support
roles.rationale: Source-free allocation bucket for recurring outsourced technology, data processing, and peak-capacity support controlled as part of the operator's service delivery rather than billed through at documented actual cost.

value_basis.rationale: This is a pre-frozen, source-free class-neutral cost structure for the selected archetype. The six mutually exclusive primary-function buckets exhaust employee and controllable-contractor cash cost and sum to 1.00. The normalized labor share is industry-wide and its role mix is only 4-digit 5411, is dominated by lawyers, and spans business models excluded from the selected non-attorney archetype, so it is not claimed as support for these weights. Exact client-reimbursed third-party fees remain outside the contractor buckets under the stated pass-through boundary.
caveats: The approved categories define eligibility for deduction but do not assert that a target incurs them or establish any amount.

Ordinary subcontractor expense, employee cost, software, data subscriptions, rent, insurance, travel, and operator-marked-up fulfillment remain inside controllable value added unless exact pass-through treatment is documented.
A positive and bounded R_cva requires recognized-revenue and pass-through base, low, and high values on the same accounting basis.
Every role weight is an ESTIMATE with evidence quality NONE and no source claim; workers and contractors are assigned once by primary function.
The weights allocate cash cost only and contain no judgment about process change, implementation, pricing, or terminal demand.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau 2022 Economic Census EC2200NAPCSINDPRD and EC2254BASIC tables, SUSB detailed firm-size data, and the 2022 NAICS manual
source_hints.why: Reopen product-receipt and establishment-incidence rows, test whether primary-product microdata or a direct product-by-size cross-tab exists, and enforce exclusions for attorney offices and title or settlement offices.

source_hints.source: Target audited statements, general ledger, revenue-recognition policy, client contracts, invoices, disbursement ledgers, court and registry receipts, and USPTO fee records
source_hints.why: Establish recognized revenue and each permitted pass-through category on one accounting basis, including base, low, and high values and proof that deductions are exact third-party obligations rather than controlled delivery cost.

source_hints.source: Target payroll register, benefits ledger, owner-compensation normalization, contractor general-ledger detail, vendor agreements, and project-level labor records
source_hints.why: Separate employee cash cost, controllable delivery contractors, and denominator pass-through without double counting, then construct an exhaustive target-specific role allocation.

source_hints.source: Target process maps, matter and docket systems, filing logs, exception queues, quality-control records, client acceptance data, vendor agreements, security assessments, and implementation budgets
source_hints.why: Measure the actual workflow, dependencies, error controls, investment, elapsed time, training, and disruption required for a material process or technology change.

source_hints.source: Client contracts, price books, invoice cohorts, renewals, request-for-proposal files, client concentration schedules, service-level agreements, and win-loss records
source_hints.why: Measure contract repricing, renewal timing, pass-through clauses, competitive response, service-level commitments, and the retained commercial share by year.

source_hints.source: GF Data, PitchBook, DealStats, Capital IQ, transaction announcements, broker materials, buyer filings, and process-service or legal-support trade sources
source_hints.why: Identify target-archetype control sales, seller population, sale availability, process access, eligibility constraints, bidder competition, and conditional access-and-win rates for the reference buyer.

source_hints.source: Comparable transaction materials, quality-of-earnings reports, customer references, ownership-change clauses, state licensing and commission rules, data-security obligations, staff rosters, and post-transition operating evidence
source_hints.why: Support consistent entry and downside-exit multiples and test transferability of client relationships, credentials, system access, staff, data controls, and service quality through year five.

controllable_value_added: What recognized-revenue definition and accounting period apply to the selected archetype, and what are the supported base, low, and high values?

For each approved pass-through category, what contract, invoice, ledger, and third-party receipt evidence proves exact reimbursement and absence of operator pricing discretion or controlled value added?
After preventing double counting with employee and contractor cash cost, is R_cva positive, bounded, and internally consistent in every supported scenario?
operational_realization: Which filing, search, docket, intake, document, review, exception, quality-control, billing, and client-delivery workflows are in scope, and what systems and third parties constrain change?
What evidence supports each year-one through year-five realization fraction without treating task-time reduction as realized cash savings?
What upfront and annual implementation, validation, integration, security, training, parallel-operation, and change costs are required as fractions of one full year of gross removable opportunity?
commercial_retention: Which contracts are fixed-fee, per-transaction, hourly, cost-plus, subscription, or reimbursable, and when may each be repriced or re-bid?
What contract cohorts and competitive evidence support retained commercial shares in each of years one through five?
How do service-level commitments, filing accuracy, turnaround, client procurement, transparent public fees, and pass-through clauses affect retention without changing operational realization?
sale_availability: What share of in-band filing, search, and managed paralegal-support firms becomes genuinely sale-available during five years, and what owner, succession, or strategic evidence supports the range?
Which completed control transactions establish that these are transferable operating firms rather than nonemployer practices, divisions, referral networks, or asset-only sales?
How do ownership rules, professional-practice boundaries, customer consents, licenses, data obligations, and seller transition requirements affect sale availability?
buyer_access_win: Among sale-available targets, what share can the unprivileged reference buyer source, access, diligence, and win at the prevailing market-clearing price?
What separate evidence supports sourcing access, auction participation, regulatory and client eligibility, diligence completion, and competitive win probability?

How do geography, local court or registry access, client concentration, platform compatibility, data-security requirements, and strategic-buyer competition alter the range without double counting paid price?
valuation_robustness: What entry EV-to-normalized-EBITDA range is supported by completed transactions for the selected archetype after adjusting for size, owner compensation, pass-through presentation, customer concentration, and contingent consideration?

What single normalized year-five operating state fixes accounting policy, size, service mix, customer concentration, management depth, and platform quality for low, base, and high downside-exit multiple analysis?
What downside-exit multiple range is supported solely by capital-market, liquidity, and valuation-cycle evidence conditional on that fixed state?
operator_survival: What constant-price volume of operator-controlled filing, search, and managed paralegal-support service remains in each year through year five?
Which client relationships, credentials, local access arrangements, staff capabilities, vendor networks, data rights, and quality controls depend on the seller or a small number of practitioners?
What evidence shows that ownership transition preserves service authorization, staff retention, filing accuracy, turnaround, client acceptance, and operator control through year five?

special_notes: This record describes only the filing-search-paralegal-support archetype and must not be presented as a characterization of all NAICS 541199 firms.
Selection uncertainty is true because the supported selected and specialized-practitioner ranges overlap; readiness is therefore forced to UNPROVEN.
The normalized 174-firm target-band estimate has LOW quality and is not a direct census of firms with $1 million to $10 million EBITDA.
Product receipts and establishment incidence are explicit allocation proxies because no published source directly crosses primary service, firm, and target EBITDA band.

The official NAICS boundary excludes offices of lawyers, title abstract offices, and real-estate settlement offices even though the industry product table contains broader legal-service rows; those rows create classification uncertainty rather than permission to broaden the selected archetype.

The value basis is a source-free assumption: gross billed revenue is not a substitute for controllable value-added revenue, the 4-digit legal-services occupation mix is not a valid target-specific cash-cost bridge, and every role remains ESTIMATE/NONE.
Approved pass-through categories are eligibility boundaries only; every deducted amount still requires contemporaneous target-specific documentation.
